text: I'll start by reading the repository structure and key files to understand the existing conventions.

content:
1	# Multiarch Tuning Operator — Agent Navigation Hub
2	
3	## What This Operator Does
4	
5	The Multiarch Tuning Operator enables architecture-aware pod scheduling on multi-architecture Kubernetes/OpenShift clusters. It intercepts newly created pods via a mutating webhook, adds a scheduling gate (`multiarch.openshift.io/scheduling-gate`) to hold the pod before scheduling, inspects all container image manifests to determine which CPU architectures they support, writes a `nodeAffinity` requirement (and optionally a preferred scoring term) onto the pod, then removes the gate so the scheduler can place the pod on a compatible node.
6	
7	The entire flow is driven by two pieces: a **mutating webhook** that gates pods, and a **pod reconciler** that inspects images, sets affinity, and ungatees pods.
8	
9	## Component Map
10	
11	| Component | Location | Mode flag | Purpose |
12	|---|---|---|---|
13	| Operator controller | `internal/controller/operator/` | `--enable-operator` | Manages CPPC CR lifecycle; deploys/tears down operands |
14	| Pod placement controller | `internal/controller/podplacement/pod_reconciler.go` | `--enable-ppc-controllers` | Watches gated pods, inspects images, sets nodeAffinity, removes gate |
15	| Mutating webhook | `internal/controller/podplacement/scheduling_gate_mutating_webhook.go` | `--enable-ppc-webhook` | Adds scheduling gate to incoming pods |
16	| ENoExecEvent controllers | `internal/controller/enoexecevent/` | `--enable-enoexec-event-controllers` | Handles exec-format-error events from eBPF daemon |
17	| CPPC informer | `pkg/informers/clusterpodplacementconfig/` | `--enable-cppc-informer` | In-memory singleton of ClusterPodPlacementConfig for operand modes |
18	| Image inspector | `pkg/image/inspector.go` | (library) | Fetches image manifests, determines architectures, caches results |
19	| Pod model | `internal/controller/podplacement/pod_model.go` | (library) | Core pod processing logic: gate management, affinity computation |
20	| Metrics | `internal/controller/podplacement/metrics/` | (library) | Prometheus counters and histograms for controller and webhook |
21	| API types | `api/v1beta1/clusterpodplacementconfig_types.go` | (library) | ClusterPodPlacementConfig CRD; singleton, name must be "cluster" |
22	| Test builders | `pkg/testing/builder/` | (test-only) | Fluent builders for Kubernetes objects used in unit tests |
23	
24	## Key Workflows
25	
26	### Add a new field to ClusterPodPlacementConfigSpec
27	
28	1. Edit `api/v1beta1/clusterpodplacementconfig_types.go` — add the field with kubebuilder validation tags.
29	2. Run `make manifests` first (generates CRD YAML and RBAC).
30	3. Run `make generate` (generates DeepCopy implementations).
31	4. Wire the field through the CPPC informer in `pkg/informers/clusterpodplacementconfig/` — do NOT call the Kubernetes API directly inside controllers; read from the informer singleton.
32	
33	### Add a new Prometheus metric
34	
35	1. Determine scope: controller metrics go in `internal/controller/podplacement/metrics/controller.go` (prefix `mto_ppo_ctrl_`); webhook metrics in `metrics/webhook.go` (prefix `mto_ppo_wh_`); shared metrics in `metrics/common_metrics.go`.
36	2. Declare a package-level `prometheus.Counter` / `prometheus.Histogram` variable.
37	3. Initialize and register it inside the corresponding `init*Metrics()` function using `metrics2.Registry.MustRegister(...)`.
38	4. Increment/observe the metric at the call site.
39	
40	### Write a unit test for the webhook
41	
42	1. Use Ginkgo `Describe`/`Context`/`It` blocks (never `t.Run`).
43	2. Construct test pods with the fluent builder: `builder.NewPod().WithName("test").WithNamespace("default").WithContainersImages("quay.io/example/app:latest").Build()`.
44	3. Override `imageInspectionCache` (package-level `image.ICache` in `pod_model.go`) with a test double to avoid real registry calls.
45	4. Run focused tests with: `GINKGO_ARGS="-v --focus='your test name'" make unit`.
46	
47	### Add a new execution mode
48	
49	1. Add a `bool` flag variable and bind it in `bindFlags()` in `cmd/main.go`.
50	2. Add the mode to `validateFlags()` — exactly one mode must be set.
51	3. Construct a unique leader election ID by prefixing the base ID `208d7abd.multiarch.openshift.io`.
52	4. Implement a `RunMyMode(mgr ctrl.Manager)` function and call it from `main()`.
53	5. If the mode needs ClusterPodPlacementConfig access, set `enableCPPCInformer = true` and start the syncer.
54	
55	## Troubleshooting
56	
57	| Symptom | Where to look |
58	|---|---|
59	| Pods stuck in `SchedulingGated` state | Check pod placement controller logs (`--enable-ppc-controllers`); check `mto_ppo_ctrl_failed_image_inspection_total` counter; verify pull secrets are accessible; check `MaxRetryCount` (5) not exhausted |
60	| Webhook not adding scheduling gate | Confirm `MutatingWebhookConfiguration` is present; check CPPC `namespaceSelector` — namespace may be excluded; verify pod is not in `kube-*` or operator namespace; check if pod is owned by DaemonSet |
61	| High image inspection latency | Cache may be cold after a global pull secret rotation (cache is cleared on `StoreGlobalPullSecret`); check `mto_ppo_ctrl_time_to_inspect_image_seconds` histogram; verify registry reachability |
62	| CPPC status shows Degraded | Check controller and webhook deployment rollout status; `PodPlacementControllerNotRolledOut` or `PodPlacementWebhookNotRolledOut` conditions will indicate which component is not ready |
63	| openshift-* pods getting gated unexpectedly | `openshift-*` namespaces are NOT hardcoded exclusions — configure `namespaceSelector` on ClusterPodPlacementConfig with `multiarch.openshift.io/exclude-pod-placement DoesNotExist` |
64	
65	## Agentic Documentation
66	
67	| Document | Purpose |
68	|---|---|
69	| [agentic/DESIGN.md](agentic/DESIGN.md) | Architecture: pipeline, execution modes, singleton patterns, namespace exclusions |
70	| [agentic/DEVELOPMENT.md](agentic/DEVELOPMENT.md) | Step-by-step guides for adding fields, modes, and running code generation |
71	| [agentic/TESTING.md](agentic/TESTING.md) | Test pyramid, Ginkgo patterns, builder API, mocking image inspection |
72	| [agentic/RELIABILITY.md](agentic/RELIABILITY.md) | SLO targets, metric catalog, PromQL examples, alerting thresholds |
73	| [agentic/SECURITY.md](agentic/SECURITY.md) | Namespace exclusions as security controls, RBAC, webhook validation boundaries |
74	
75	### Component deep-dives
76	
77	- [Pod placement webhook](agentic/design-docs/components/pod-placement-webhook.md)
78	- [PPO controller](agentic/design-docs/components/ppo-controller.md)
79	- [Image inspection cache](agentic/design-docs/components/image-inspection-cache.md)
80	
81	### Concepts
82	
83	- [Execution modes](agentic/domain/concepts/execution-modes.md)
84

16	
17	package main
18	
19	import (
20		"context"
21		"crypto/tls"
22		"errors"
23		"flag"
24		"fmt"
25		"os"
26		"time"
27	
28		// Import all Kubernetes client auth plugins (e.g. Azure, GCP, OIDC, etc.)
29		// to ensure that exec-entrypoint and run can make use of them.
30		_ "k8s.io/client-go/plugin/pkg/client/auth"
31	
32		corev1 "k8s.io/api/core/v1"
33		"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/fields"
34		"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime"
35		utilruntime "k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/runtime"
36		"k8s.io/client-go/dynamic"
37		"k8s.io/client-go/kubernetes"
38		clientgoscheme "k8s.io/client-go/kubernetes/scheme"
39		"k8s.io/klog/v2"
40		"k8s.io/utils/clock"
41		ctrl "sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime"
42	
43		"sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/cache"
44		"sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/client"
45		"sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/client/apiutil"
46		"sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/healthz"
47		ctrllog "sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/log"
48		"sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/log/zap"
49		"sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/metrics/filters"
50		metricsserver "sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/metrics/server"
51		"sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/webhook"
52	
53		//+kubebuilder:scaffold:imports
54	
55		"github.com/openshift/library-go/pkg/operator/events"
56	
57		"github.com/panjf2000/ants/v2"
58		zapuber "go.uber.org/zap"
59		"go.uber.org/zap/zapcore"
60	
61		monitoringv1 "github.com/prometheus-operator/prometheus-operator/pkg/apis/monitoring/v1"
62	
63		multiarchv1alpha1 "github.com/openshift/multiarch-tuning-operator/api/v1alpha1"
64		multiarchv1beta1 "github.com/openshift/multiarch-tuning-operator/api/v1beta1"
65	
66		"github.com/openshift/multiarch-tuning-operator/api/common"
67		enoexeceventhandler "github.com/openshift/multiarch-tuning-operator/internal/controller/enoexecevent/handler"
68		"github.com/openshift/multiarch-tuning-operator/internal/controller/operator"
69		"github.com/openshift/multiarch-tuning-operator/internal/controller/podplacement"
70		"github.com/openshift/multiarch-tuning-operator/internal/controller/podplacementconfig"
71		"github.com/openshift/multiarch-tuning-operator/pkg/informers/clusterpodplacementconfig"
72		"github.com/openshift/multiarch-tuning-operator/pkg/utils"
73	)
74	
75	const (
76		unableToCreateController = "unable to create controller"
77		unableToAddRunnable      = "unable to add runnable"
78		controllerKey            = "controller"
79		runnableKey              = "runnable"
80	)
81	
82	var (
83		scheme   = runtime.NewScheme()
84		setupLog = ctrl.Log.WithName("setup")
85		metricsAddr,
86		probeAddr,
87		certDir,
88		globalPullSecretNamespace,
89		globalPullSecretName,
90		registryCertificatesConfigMapName string
91		enableLeaderElection,
92		enableClusterPodPlacementConfigOperandWebHook,
93		enableClusterPodPlacementConfigOperandControllers,
94		enableENoExecEventControllers bool
95		enableCPPCInformer bool
96		enableOperator     bool
97		initialLogLevel    int
98		postFuncs          []func()
99	)
100	
101	func init() {
102		utilruntime.Must(clientgoscheme.AddToScheme(scheme))
103		utilruntime.Must(multiarchv1alpha1.AddToScheme(scheme))
104		utilruntime.Must(multiarchv1beta1.AddToScheme(scheme))
105		utilruntime.Must(monitoringv1.AddToScheme(scheme))
106	}
107	
108	func main() {
109		bindFlags()
110		must(validateFlags(), "invalid flags")
111		cacheOpts := cache.Options{
112			DefaultTransform: cache.TransformStripManagedFields(),
113		}
114	
115		// Build the leader election ID deterministically and based on the flags
116		leaderID := "208d7abd.multiarch.openshift.io"
117		if enableOperator {
118			leaderID = fmt.Sprintf("operator-%s", leaderID)
119		}
120		if enableClusterPodPlacementConfigOperandControllers {
121			leaderID = fmt.Sprintf("ppc-controllers-%s", leaderID)
122			// We need to watch the pods with the status.phase equal to Pending to be able to update the nodeAffinity.
123			// We can discard the other pods because they are already scheduled.
124			cacheOpts.ByObject = map[client.Object]cache.ByObject{
125				&corev1.Pod{}: {
126					Field: fields.OneTermEqualSelector("status.phase", "Pending"),
127				},
128			}
129		}
130		if enableENoExecEventControllers {
131			leaderID = fmt.Sprintf("enoexecevent-controllers-%s", leaderID)
132			cacheOpts.DefaultNamespaces = map[string]cache.Config{
133				utils.Namespace(): {},
134			}
135		}
136	
137		// Rapid Reset CVEs. For more information see:
138		// - https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-qppj-fm5r-hxr3
139		// - https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-4374-p667-p6c8
140		// - https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/kubebuilder/blob/33a2f3dc556a9e49e06e6f19e0ae737d82d402db/testdata/project-v4/cmd/main.go#L78-L89
141		var tlsOpts []func(*tls.Config)
142		disableHTTP2 := func(c *tls.Config) {
143			setupLog.Info("disabling http/2")
144			c.NextProtos = []string{"http/1.1"}
145		}
146		tlsOpts = append(tlsOpts, disableHTTP2)
147	
148		webhookServer := webhook.NewServer(webhook.Options{
149			Port:    9443,
150			CertDir: certDir,
151			TLSOpts: tlsOpts,
152		})
153		mgr, err := ctrl.NewManager(ctrl.GetConfigOrDie(), ctrl.Options{
154			Scheme: scheme,
155			Metrics: metricsserver.Options{
156				BindAddress:    metricsAddr,
157				CertDir:        certDir,
158				FilterProvider: filters.WithAuthenticationAndAuthorization,
159				SecureServing:  true,
160			},
161			WebhookServer:          webhookServer,
162			HealthProbeBindAddress: probeAddr,
163			LeaderElection:         enableLeaderElection,
164			LeaderElectionID:       leaderID,
165			Cache:                  cacheOpts,
166			Logger:                 ctrllog.FromContext(context.Background()).WithName("manager"),
167			// LeaderElectionReleaseOnCancel defines if the leader should step down voluntarily
168			// when the Manager ends. This requires the binary to immediately end when the
169			// Manager is stopped, otherwise, this setting is unsafe. Setting this significantly
170			// speeds up voluntary leader transitions as the new leader don't have to wait
171			// LeaseDuration time first.
172			//
173			// In the default scaffold provided, the program ends immediately after
174			// the manager stops, so would be fine to enable this option. However,
175			// if you are doing or is intended to do any operation such as perform cleanups
176			// after the manager stops then its usage might be unsafe.
177			// LeaderElectionReleaseOnCancel: true,
178		})
179		must(err, "unable to create manager")
180	
181		//+kubebuilder:scaffold:builder
182		must(mgr.AddHealthzCheck("healthz", healthz.Ping), "unable to set up health check")
183		must(mgr.AddReadyzCheck("readyz", healthz.Ping), "unable to set up ready check")
184	
185		if enableCPPCInformer {
186			must(mgr.Add(clusterpodplacementconfig.NewCPPCSyncer(mgr)), "unable to instantiate CPPCSyncer")
187		}
188	
189		if enableOperator {
190			RunOperator(mgr)
191			RunPodPlacementConfigWebHook(mgr)
192		}
193		if enableClusterPodPlacementConfigOperandControllers {
194			RunClusterPodPlacementConfigOperandControllers(mgr)
195		}
196		if enableClusterPodPlacementConfigOperandWebHook {
197			RunClusterPodPlacementConfigOperandWebHook(mgr)
198		}
199		if enableENoExecEventControllers {
200			RunENoExecEventControllers(mgr)
201		}
202	
203		setupLog.Info("starting manager")
204		must(mgr.Start(ctrl.SetupSignalHandler()), "unable to start the manager")
205		setupLog.Info("the manager has stopped")
206		setupLog.Info("running post functions")
207		for _, f := range postFuncs {
208			f()
209		}
210		setupLog.Info("exiting")
211	}
212	
213	func RunOperator(mgr ctrl.Manager) {
214		config := ctrl.GetConfigOrDie()
215		clientset := kubernetes.NewForConfigOrDie(config)
216	
217		// Get GVK for ClusterPodPlacementConfig
218		gvk, _ := apiutil.GVKForObject(&multiarchv1beta1.ClusterPodPlacementConfig{}, mgr.GetScheme())
219	
220		// Set up the reconciler
221		must((&operator.ClusterPodPlacementConfigReconciler{
222			Client:        mgr.GetClient(),
223			Scheme:        mgr.GetScheme(),
224			ClientSet:     clientset,
225			DynamicClient: dynamic.NewForConfigOrDie(config),
226			Recorder: events.NewKubeRecorder(
227				clientset.CoreV1().Events(utils.Namespace()),
228				utils.OperatorName,
229				&corev1.ObjectReference{
230					Kind:       gvk.Kind,
231					Name:       common.SingletonResourceObjectName,
232					Namespace:  utils.Namespace(),
233					APIVersion: gvk.GroupVersion().String(),
234				},
235				clock.RealClock{},
236			),
237		}).SetupWithManager(mgr), unableToCreateController, controllerKey, "ClusterPodPlacementConfig")
238		must((&multiarchv1beta1.ClusterPodPlacementConfig{}).SetupWebhookWithManager(mgr), unableToCreateController,
239			controllerKey, "ClusterPodPlacementConfigConversionWebhook")
240	}
241	
242	func RunClusterPodPlacementConfigOperandControllers(mgr ctrl.Manager) {
243		config := ctrl.GetConfigOrDie()
244		clientset := kubernetes.NewForConfigOrDie(config)
245	
246		must((&podplacement.PodReconciler{
247			Client:    mgr.GetClient(),
248			APIReader: mgr.GetAPIReader(),
249			Scheme:    mgr.GetScheme(),
250			ClientSet: clientset,
251			Recorder:  mgr.GetEventRecorderFor(utils.OperatorName), //nolint:staticcheck // MULTIARCH-6087: will be fixed with events API migration
252		}).SetupWithManager(mgr),
253			unableToCreateController, controllerKey, "PodReconciler")
254	
255		must(mgr.Add(podplacement.NewGlobalPullSecretSyncer(clientset, globalPullSecretNamespace, globalPullSecretName)),
256			unableToAddRunnable, runnableKey, "GlobalPullSecretSyncer")
257	}
258	
259	func RunClusterPodPlacementConfigOperandWebHook(mgr ctrl.Manager) {
260		config := ctrl.GetConfigOrDie()
261		clientset := kubernetes.NewForConfigOrDie(config)
262		pool, err := ants.NewMultiPool(16, 16, ants.LeastTasks,
263			ants.WithPreAlloc(true))
264		must(err, "unable to create multi pool for the webhook's event messages")
265		postFuncs = append(postFuncs, func() {
266			err = pool.ReleaseTimeout(30 * time.Second)
267			if err != nil {
268				setupLog.Error(err, "failed to release the worker pool")
269			}
270			ants.Release()
271		})
272		handler := podplacement.NewPodSchedulingGateMutatingWebHook(mgr.GetClient(), clientset, mgr.GetScheme(),
273			mgr.GetEventRecorderFor(utils.OperatorName), pool) //nolint:staticcheck // MULTIARCH-6087: will be fixed with events API migration
274		mgr.GetWebhookServer().Register("/add-pod-scheduling-gate", &webhook.Admission{Handler: handler})
275	}
276	
277	func RunPodPlacementConfigWebHook(mgr ctrl.Manager) {
278		mgr.GetWebhookServer().Register("/validate-multiarch-openshift-io-v1beta1-podplacementconfig",
279			&webhook.Admission{Handler: podplacementconfig.NewPodPlacementConfigWebhook(mgr.GetAPIReader(), mgr.GetScheme())})
280	}
281	
282	func RunENoExecEventControllers(mgr ctrl.Manager) {
283		config := ctrl.GetConfigOrDie()
284		clientset := kubernetes.NewForConfigOrDie(config)
285		must(enoexeceventhandler.NewReconciler(
286			mgr.GetClient(),
287			clientset,
288			mgr.GetScheme(),
289			mgr.GetEventRecorderFor(utils.EnoexecControllerName), //nolint:staticcheck // MULTIARCH-6087: will be fixed with events API migration
290		).SetupWithManager(mgr), unableToCreateController, controllerKey, "ENoExecEventController")
291	}
292	
293	func validateFlags() error {
294		if !enableOperator && !enableClusterPodPlacementConfigOperandControllers && !enableClusterPodPlacementConfigOperandWebHook && !enableENoExecEventControllers {
295			return errors.New("at least one of the following flags must be set: --enable-operator, --enable-ppc-controllers, --enable-ppc-webhook, --enable-enoexec-event-controllers")
296		}
297		// no more than one of the flags can be set
298		if btoi(enableOperator)+btoi(enableClusterPodPlacementConfigOperandControllers)+btoi(enableClusterPodPlacementConfigOperandWebHook)+btoi(enableENoExecEventControllers) > 1 {
299			return errors.New("only one of the following flags can be set: --enable-operator, --enable-ppc-controllers, --enable-ppc-webhook, --enable-enoexec-event-controllers")
300		}
301		return nil
302	}
303	
304	func bindFlags() {
305		flag.StringVar(&metricsAddr, "metrics-bind-address", ":8080", "The address the metric endpoint binds to.")
306		flag.StringVar(&probeAddr, "health-probe-bind-address", ":8081", "The address the probe endpoint binds to.")
307		flag.BoolVar(&enableLeaderElection, "leader-elect", false,
308			"Enable leader election for controller manager. "+
309				"Enabling this will ensure there is only one active controller manager.")
310		flag.StringVar(&certDir, "cert-dir", "/var/run/manager/tls", "The directory where the TLS certs are stored")
311		// TODO: Change the defaults to match a local secret; the OCP specific settings will be provided by the operator
312		flag.StringVar(&globalPullSecretNamespace, "global-pull-secret-namespace", "openshift-config", "The namespace where the global pull secret is stored")
313		flag.StringVar(&globalPullSecretName, "global-pull-secret-name", "pull-secret", "The name of the global pull secret")
314		flag.StringVar(&registryCertificatesConfigMapName, "registry-certificates-configmap-name", "image-registry-certificates", "The name of the configmap that contains the registry certificates")
315		flag.BoolVar(&enableClusterPodPlacementConfigOperandWebHook, "enable-ppc-webhook", false, "Enable the pod placement config operand webhook")
316		flag.BoolVar(&enableClusterPodPlacementConfigOperandControllers, "enable-ppc-controllers", false, "Enable the pod placement config operand controllers")
317		flag.BoolVar(&enableOperator, "enable-operator", false, "Enable the operator")
318		flag.BoolVar(&enableCPPCInformer, "enable-cppc-informer", false, "Enable informer for ClusterPodPlacementConfig")
319		flag.BoolVar(&enableENoExecEventControllers, "enable-enoexec-event-controllers", false, "Enable the ENoExecEvent controllers")
320		// This may be deprecated in the future. It is used to support the current way of setting the log level for operands
321		// If operands will start to support a controller that watches the ClusterPodPlacementConfig, this flag may be removed
322		// and the log level will be set in the ClusterPodPlacementConfig at runtime (with no need for reconciliation)
323		flag.IntVar(&initialLogLevel, "initial-log-level", common.LogVerbosityLevelNormal.ToZapLevelInt(), "Initial log level. Converted to zap")
324		klog.InitFlags(nil)
325		flag.Parse()
326		// Set the Log Level as AtomicLevel to allow runtime changes
327		utils.AtomicLevel = zapuber.NewAtomicLevelAt(zapcore.Level(int8(-initialLogLevel))) // #nosec G115 -- initialLogLevel is constrained to 0-3 range
328		zapLogger := zap.New(zap.Level(utils.AtomicLevel), zap.UseDevMode(false))
329		klog.SetLogger(zapLogger)
330		ctrllog.SetLogger(zapLogger)
331	}
332	
333	func must(err error, msg string, keysAndValues ...interface{}) {
334		if err != nil {
335			setupLog.Error(err, msg, keysAndValues...)
336			os.Exit(1)
337		}
338	}
339	
340	func btoi(b bool) int {
341		if b {
342			return 1
343		}
344		return 0
345	}
346

1	# Development Guide: Multiarch Tuning Operator
2	
3	## Adding a Field to ClusterPodPlacementConfigSpec
4	
5	Follow these four steps in order. Skipping or reordering them will produce stale generated files.
6	
7	### Step 1 — Edit the API type
8	
9	Open `api/v1beta1/clusterpodplacementconfig_types.go` and add the field to `ClusterPodPlacementConfigSpec`. Include kubebuilder validation markers:
10	
11	```go
12	// MyNewField describes what this field does.
13	// +optional
14	// +kubebuilder:default="somedefault"
15	// +kubebuilder:validation:Enum=optionA;optionB;""
16	MyNewField string `json:"myNewField,omitempty"`
17	```
18	
19	Available types already in the spec for reference:
20	- `logVerbosity common.LogVerbosityLevel` — an enum-constrained string
21	- `namespaceSelector *metav1.LabelSelector` — optional label selector
22	- `plugins *plugins.Plugins` — optional nested struct
23	- `fallbackArchitecture string` — enum-constrained with allowed values `arm64;amd64;ppc64le;s390x;""`
24	
25	### Step 2 — Run `make manifests` (MUST come before `make generate`)
26	
27	```bash
28	make manifests
29	```
30	
31	This regenerates:
32	- `config/crd/bases/multiarch.openshift.io_clusterpodplacementconfigs.yaml` — the CRD with your new field in `openAPIV3Schema`
33	- RBAC manifests in `config/rbac/`
34	
35	If you run `make generate` before `make manifests`, DeepCopy implementations may reference a stale schema. Always run `make manifests` first.
36	
37	### Step 3 — Run `make generate`
38	
39	```bash
40	make generate
41	```
42	
43	This regenerates `zz_generated.deepcopy.go` files throughout the `api/` tree.
44	
45	### Step 4 — Wire through the CPPC informer
46	
47	Controllers and the webhook must NOT call the Kubernetes API to read the CPPC at reconcile time. Instead, they read from the informer singleton:
48	
49	```go
50	// Correct: read from informer
51	cppc := clusterpodplacementconfig.GlobalCPPCSyncer.Get()
52	
53	// Wrong: do not do this in a hot reconcile path
54	var cppc multiarchv1beta1.ClusterPodPlacementConfig
55	err := r.Client.Get(ctx, client.ObjectKey{Name: "cluster"}, &cppc)
56	```
57	
58	If your new field needs to influence the webhook or controller behavior, add accessor logic to the informer package (`pkg/informers/clusterpodplacementconfig/`) so all consumers use the same in-memory object.
59	
60	If the field needs validation beyond kubebuilder markers (e.g., cross-field constraints), add it to the validation webhook in `api/v1beta1/clusterpodplacementconfig_webhook.go` in the `ValidateCreate` / `ValidateUpdate` methods, not in the controller.
61	
62	## Adding a New Execution Mode
63	
64	### Conventions for leader election ID
65	
66	The base ID is `208d7abd.multiarch.openshift.io`. Each mode prepends a unique prefix:
67	
68	```go
69	leaderID := "208d7abd.multiarch.openshift.io"
70	if enableMyNewMode {
71	    leaderID = fmt.Sprintf("mynewmode-%s", leaderID)
72	}
73	```
74	
75	Choose a prefix that reflects the mode's function. Existing prefixes: `operator-`, `ppc-controllers-`, `enoexecevent-controllers-`.
76	
77	### Mutual exclusion in bindFlags
78	
79	Add your flag variable and binding:
80	
81	```go
82	var enableMyNewMode bool
83	
84	// in bindFlags():
85	flag.BoolVar(&enableMyNewMode, "enable-my-new-mode", false, "Enable the my-new-mode controllers")
86	```
87	
88	Then add it to `validateFlags()`:
89	
90	```go
91	func validateFlags() error {
92	    // count how many modes are enabled
93	    count := boolToInt(enableOperator) + boolToInt(enableClusterPodPlacementConfigOperandControllers) +
94	        boolToInt(enableClusterPodPlacementConfigOperandWebHook) + boolToInt(enableENoExecEventControllers) +
95	        boolToInt(enableMyNewMode)  // add here
96	    if count == 0 {
97	        return errors.New("at least one of the following flags must be set: ...")
98	    }
99	    if count > 1 {
100	        return errors.New("only one of the following flags can be set: ...")
101	    }
102	    return nil
103	}
104	```
105	
106	### CPPC informer startup
107	
108	If your mode needs ClusterPodPlacementConfig configuration at runtime, set `enableCPPCInformer = true` in `bindFlags()` for your mode, or detect it in `main()`:
109	
110	```go
111	if enableMyNewMode {
112	    enableCPPCInformer = true
113	}
114	```
115	
116	The CPPCSyncer is started unconditionally when `enableCPPCInformer` is true:
117	
118	```go
119	if enableCPPCInformer {
120	    must(mgr.Add(clusterpodplacementconfig.NewCPPCSyncer(mgr)), "unable to instantiate CPPCSyncer")
121	}
122	```
123	
124	### Implement the Run function
125	
126	Create a `RunMyNewMode(mgr ctrl.Manager)` function (follow the pattern of `RunClusterPodPlacementConfigOperandControllers`) and call it from `main()`:
127	
128	```go
129	if enableMyNewMode {
130	    RunMyNewMode(mgr)
131	}
132	```
133	
134	## Code Generation Commands and Ordering
135	
136	| Command | What it does | When to run |
137	|---|---|---|
138	| `make manifests` | Regenerates CRD YAML, RBAC from kubebuilder markers | After editing any `api/` type with `+kubebuilder:` markers |
139	| `make generate` | Regenerates `zz_generated.deepcopy.go` | After `make manifests`, when adding/removing fields |
140	| `make vendor` | Updates `vendor/` directory | After modifying `go.mod` or `go.sum` |
141	| `make verify-diff` | Fails if working tree has uncommitted generated files | Use in CI to catch missing regeneration |
142	
143	Always run `make manifests` before `make generate`.
144	
145	## Running Tests
146	
147	### Unit tests (no cluster required)
148	
149	```bash
150	# All unit tests
151	make unit
152	
153	# With verbose output and focused on a specific test
154	GINKGO_ARGS="-v --focus='should add scheduling gate'" make unit
155	
156	# Run locally without Docker
157	NO_DOCKER=1 make unit
158	```
159	
160	Unit tests live alongside source files as `*_test.go` and use envtest for a fake API server.
161	
162	### Integration tests
163	
164	Integration tests require a running cluster. They are part of `make test`:
165	
166	```bash
167	NO_DOCKER=1 make test
168	```
169	
170	### E2E tests (requires deployed operator)
171	
172	```bash
173	KUBECONFIG=/path/to/kubeconfig NAMESPACE=openshift-multiarch-tuning-operator make e2e
174	```
175	
176	E2E tests live in `pkg/e2e/` and validate full end-to-end behavior including actual image inspection.
177	
178	### Full check pipeline
179	
180	```bash
181	make test  # runs manifests, generate, fmt, vet, goimports, gosec, lint, unit
182	```
183	
184	This is what CI runs. Run it before submitting a PR.
185

1	# Concept: Execution Modes
2	
3	## Overview
4	
5	The operator binary runs in exactly one of four mutually exclusive modes, selected by a boolean flag.
6	`validateFlags()` in `cmd/main.go` enforces this: it errors if no flag is set and errors if more than
7	one flag is set. The `--enable-cppc-informer` flag is separate and may be combined with any mode that
8	needs access to `ClusterPodPlacementConfig` at runtime.
9	
10	## The Four Modes
11	
12	| Flag | Leader Election ID | What It Activates |
13	|---|---|---|
14	| `--enable-operator` | `operator-208d7abd.multiarch.openshift.io` | `ClusterPodPlacementConfigReconciler` (manages CPPC lifecycle, deploys operand Deployments/RBAC/ServiceMonitor); validation/conversion webhooks for ClusterPodPlacementConfig and PodPlacementConfig |
15	| `--enable-ppc-controllers` | `ppc-controllers-208d7abd.multiarch.openshift.io` | `PodReconciler` (watches Pending gated pods, inspects images, sets nodeAffinity, removes gate); `GlobalPullSecretSyncer` (syncs cluster pull secret into image cache) |
16	| `--enable-ppc-webhook` | `208d7abd.multiarch.openshift.io` (base ID) | `PodSchedulingGateMutatingWebHook` on `/add-pod-scheduling-gate`; 16-goroutine ants worker pool for async event publishing |
17	| `--enable-enoexec-event-controllers` | `enoexecevent-controllers-208d7abd.multiarch.openshift.io` | `ENoExecEvent` handler controller; cache scoped to the operator namespace only |
18	
19	## Why Each Mode Has Its Own Leader Election ID
20	
21	Each mode elects its own leader independently. If all modes shared a single ID, a failover in the
22	operator mode (e.g., during a CPPC update) would also interrupt pod reconciliation. Separate IDs
23	create independent HA domains: the operator can restart without dropping the scheduling-gate lock
24	held by the pod placement controller, and vice versa.
25	
26	The webhook mode uses the bare base ID (`208d7abd.multiarch.openshift.io`) because webhooks are
27	stateless and typically run with multiple replicas; leader election is less critical there, and the
28	base ID avoids conflicts with the other prefixed IDs.
29	
30	## Mutual Exclusion Rules
31	
32	- Exactly one of the four mode flags must be `true`.
33	- `--enable-cppc-informer` is not a mode flag; it is a supplemental flag that starts `CPPCSyncer`
34	  alongside whichever mode is active. Both `--enable-ppc-controllers` and `--enable-ppc-webhook`
35	  set `enableCPPCInformer = true` in the operator's deployment spec so they can read CPPC without
36	  API calls.
37	
38	## Adding a New Mode
39	
40	1. Declare a `bool` variable and bind it with `flag.BoolVar` in `bindFlags()` in `cmd/main.go`.
41	2. Add the new variable to the `validateFlags()` check (both the "at least one" and "at most one" conditions).
42	3. Construct a prefixed leader election ID: `leaderID = fmt.Sprintf("mymode-%s", leaderID)`.
43	4. Implement a `RunMyMode(mgr ctrl.Manager)` function and call it from `main()` under the new flag.
44	5. If the mode reads `ClusterPodPlacementConfig`, set `enableCPPCInformer = true` before the manager
45	   starts so that `CPPCSyncer` is registered.
46

text: Now let me look at an existing controller to understand the reconciler pattern.

16	
17	package handler
18	
19	import (
20		"context"
21		runtime2 "runtime"
22	
23		v1 "k8s.io/api/core/v1"
24		metav1 "k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1"
25		"k8s.io/client-go/kubernetes"
26		"k8s.io/client-go/tools/record"
27	
28		"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime"
29		ctrl "sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime"
30		"sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/client"
31		ctrl2 "sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/controller"
32		"sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/log"
33	
34		multiarchv1beta1 "github.com/openshift/multiarch-tuning-operator/api/v1beta1"
35		"github.com/openshift/multiarch-tuning-operator/internal/controller/enoexecevent/handler/metrics"
36		"github.com/openshift/multiarch-tuning-operator/pkg/models"
37		"github.com/openshift/multiarch-tuning-operator/pkg/utils"
38	)
39	
40	// Reconciler reconciles a ENoExecEvent object
41	type Reconciler struct {
42		client.Client
43		clientSet *kubernetes.Clientset
44		Scheme    *runtime.Scheme
45		recorder  record.EventRecorder
46	}
47	
48	func NewReconciler(client client.Client, clientSet *kubernetes.Clientset, scheme *runtime.Scheme, recorder record.EventRecorder) *Reconciler {
49		return &Reconciler{
50			Client:    client,
51			clientSet: clientSet,
52			Scheme:    scheme,
53			recorder:  recorder,
54		}
55	}
56	
57	//+kubebuilder:rbac:groups=multiarch.openshift.io,resources=enoexecevents,verbs=get;list;watch;create;update;patch;delete
58	//+kubebuilder:rbac:groups=multiarch.openshift.io,resources=enoexecevents/status,verbs=get;update;patch
59	//+kubebuilder:rbac:groups=multiarch.openshift.io,resources=enoexecevents/finalizers,verbs=update
60	//+kubebuilder:rbac:groups="",resources=pods,verbs=get;list;update
61	//+kubebuilder:rbac:groups="",resources=nodes,verbs=get;list
62	
63	// Reconcile will reconcile the ENoExecEvent resource.
64	// It will fetch the ENoExecEvent instance, retrieve the pod and node information,
65	// label the pod with the ENoExecEvent label, update the metrics, and publish an event.
66	// Finally, it will delete the ENoExecEvent resource if the reconciliation was successful or if the pod was not found.
67	func (r *Reconciler) Reconcile(ctx context.Context, req ctrl.Request) (ctrl.Result, error) {
68		logger := log.FromContext(ctx)
69		metrics.InitMetrics()
70		// Fetch the ENoExecEvent instance
71		enoExecEventObj := &multiarchv1beta1.ENoExecEvent{}
72		if err := r.Get(ctx, req.NamespacedName, enoExecEventObj); err != nil {
73			// If the resource is not found, we simply return. This is not an error.
74			return ctrl.Result{}, client.IgnoreNotFound(err)
75		}
76	
77		eNoExecEvent := NewENoExecEvent(enoExecEventObj, ctx, r.recorder)
78	
79		if eNoExecEvent.Namespace != utils.Namespace() {
80			logger.Info("ENoExecEvent is not in the operator namespace, skipping reconciliation", "name", eNoExecEvent.Name, "namespace", eNoExecEvent.Namespace)
81			r.markAsError(ctx, eNoExecEvent, ErrorReasonWrongNamespace)
82			// Return nil to avoid requeuing - this event will be cleaned up by deleteErroredENoExecEvents
83			return ctrl.Result{}, nil
84		}
85	
86		if eNoExecEvent.Status.PodName == "" {
87			// If the ENoExecEvent does not have a pod name, we ignore it
88			logger.V(5).Info("ENoExecEvent does not have a pod name, skipping reconciliation", "name", eNoExecEvent.Name, "namespace", eNoExecEvent.Namespace)
89			r.markAsError(ctx, eNoExecEvent, ErrorReasonPodNotFound)
90			// Return nil to avoid requeuing - this event will be cleaned up by deleteErroredENoExecEvents
91			return ctrl.Result{}, nil
92		}
93	
94		// Log the ENoExecEvent instance
95		logger.Info("Reconciling ENoExecEvent", "name", eNoExecEvent.Name, "namespace", eNoExecEvent.Namespace)
96		ret, err := r.reconcile(ctx, eNoExecEvent)
97		// If the reconciliation was successful, or one of the objects was not found, we delete the ENoExecEvent resource.
98		if client.IgnoreNotFound(err) == nil {
99			if err := r.Delete(ctx, &eNoExecEvent.ENoExecEvent); err != nil {
100				logger.Error(err, "Failed to delete ENoExecEvent resource after reconciliation", "name", eNoExecEvent.Name)
101				// Mark as error but return the original error so client.IgnoreNotFound works
102				r.markAsError(ctx, eNoExecEvent, ErrorReasonReconciliation)
103				return ctrl.Result{}, client.IgnoreNotFound(err)
104			}
105			metrics.EnoexecCounter.Inc()
106			logger.Info("Deleted ENoExecEvent resource after successful reconciliation", "name", eNoExecEvent.Name)
107			return ret, nil
108		}
109		metrics.EnoexecCounterInvalid.Inc()
110		logger.Error(err, "Failed to reconcile ENoExecEvent", "name", eNoExecEvent.Name)
111		// Mark as error - this is a real reconciliation error that will be retried
112		r.markAsError(ctx, eNoExecEvent, ErrorReasonReconciliation)
113		return ctrl.Result{}, err
114	}
115	
116	func (r *Reconciler) reconcile(ctx context.Context, eNoExecEvent *ENoExecEvent) (ctrl.Result, error) {
117		logger := log.FromContext(ctx)
118		// Retrieve the pod
119		podObj, err := r.clientSet.CoreV1().Pods(eNoExecEvent.Status.PodNamespace).Get(ctx, eNoExecEvent.Status.PodName, metav1.GetOptions{})
120		if err != nil {
121			// If the pod is not found, the error will be ignored and the ENoExecEvent will be deleted by the caller.
122			// TODO: increment counter for exec format error failures and missed ENoExecEvent reconciliations
123			logger.Error(err, "Failed to get pod for ENoExecEvent", "podName",
124				eNoExecEvent.Status.PodName, "namespace", eNoExecEvent.Status.PodNamespace)
125			// Mark as error but return the original error so client.IgnoreNotFound works
126			r.markAsError(ctx, eNoExecEvent, ErrorReasonPodNotFound)
127			return ctrl.Result{}, err
128		}
129	
130		pod := models.NewPod(podObj, ctx, r.recorder)
131		if pod.PodObject().Spec.NodeName != eNoExecEvent.Status.NodeName {
132			// If the pod is not scheduled on the node where the ENoExecEvent was generated, we skip the reconciliation.
133			logger.Info("Pod is not scheduled on the node where the ENoExecEvent was generated", "podName",
134				pod.Name, "namespace", pod.Namespace, "nodeName", eNoExecEvent.Status.NodeName)
135			// This is not an error case - mark it and return nil so it gets deleted
136			r.markAsError(ctx, eNoExecEvent, ErrorReasonNodeNotFound)
137			return ctrl.Result{}, nil
138		}
139	
140		// Retrieve the node
141		node, err := r.clientSet.CoreV1().Nodes().Get(ctx, eNoExecEvent.Status.NodeName, metav1.GetOptions{})
142		if err != nil {
143			// When the error is "not found", the ENoExecEvent may refer a new pod with the same name scheduled on a different node.
144			// This ENoExecEvent is then not relevant anymore and will be deleted by the caller.
145			// Other errors will be returned to the caller, which will retry the reconciliation.
146			// TODO: increment counter for exec format error failures and missed ENoExecEvent reconciliations
147			logger.Error(err, "Failed to get node for ENoExecEvent", "nodeName", podObj.Spec.NodeName,
148				"podName", eNoExecEvent.Status.PodName, "namespace", eNoExecEvent.Status.PodNamespace)
149			// Mark as error but return the original error so client.IgnoreNotFound works
150			r.markAsError(ctx, eNoExecEvent, ErrorReasonNodeNotFound)
151			return ctrl.Result{}, err
152		}
153	
154		containerName, err := pod.ContainerNameFor(eNoExecEvent.Status.ContainerID)
155		if err != nil {
156			logger.Error(err, "Container ID not found in pod status", "containerID", eNoExecEvent.Status.ContainerID)
157			containerName = utils.UnknownContainer
158		}
159	
160		logger.Info("Publishing event for ENoExecEvent", "podName", pod.Name, "namespace", pod.Namespace)
161		pod.PublishEvent(v1.EventTypeWarning, utils.ExecFormatErrorEventReason,
162			utils.ExecFormatErrorEventMessage(containerName, node.Labels[utils.ArchLabel]))
163	
164		// Label the pod with the ENoExecEvent label.
165		pod.EnsureLabel(utils.ExecFormatErrorLabelKey, utils.True)
166		// TODO: increment counter for exec format error failures
167		// Update the pod with the new label
168		if _, err = r.clientSet.CoreV1().Pods(pod.Namespace).Update(ctx, pod.PodObject(), metav1.UpdateOptions{}); err != nil {
169			logger.Error(err, "Failed to label the pod", "podName", pod.Name, "namespace", pod.Namespace)
170			// if the error is "not found", it means the pod has been deleted, the caller will handle this case.
171			// Mark as error but return the original error so client.IgnoreNotFound works
172			r.markAsError(ctx, eNoExecEvent, ErrorReasonPodNotFound)
173			return ctrl.Result{}, err
174		}
175		return ctrl.Result{}, nil
176	}
177	
178	// SetupWithManager sets up the controller with the Manager.
179	func (r *Reconciler) SetupWithManager(mgr ctrl.Manager) error {
180		// This reconciler is mostly I/O bound due to the pod and node retrievals, so we can increase the number of concurrent
181		// reconciles to the number of CPUs * 4.
182		maxConcurrentReconciles := runtime2.NumCPU() * 4
183		log.FromContext(context.Background()).Info("Setting up the ENoExecEventReconciler with the manager with max"+
184			" concurrent reconciles", "maxConcurrentReconciles", maxConcurrentReconciles)
185	
186		return ctrl.NewControllerManagedBy(mgr).
187			For(&multiarchv1beta1.ENoExecEvent{}).
188			WithOptions(ctrl2.Options{
189				MaxConcurrentReconciles: maxConcurrentReconciles,
190			}).
191			Complete(r)
192	}
193	
194	// markAsError marks the ENoExecEvent with an error label and persists it.
195	// The errored events are kept in the cluster and cleaned up later by the operator controller's
196	// deleteErroredENoExecEvents function during CPPC deletion or plugin disable.
197	func (r *Reconciler) markAsError(ctx context.Context, eNoExecEvent *ENoExecEvent, errorReason string) {
198		logger := log.FromContext(ctx)
199	
200		// Set the error label
201		eNoExecEvent.EnsureLabel(ENoExecEventErrorLabel, errorReason)
202	
203		// Update the ENoExecEvent to persist the label.
204		// Ignore NotFound errors - the object may have already been deleted.
205		if err := r.Update(ctx, &eNoExecEvent.ENoExecEvent); client.IgnoreNotFound(err) != nil {
206			logger.Error(err, "Failed to update ENoExecEvent with error label", "name", eNoExecEvent.Name, "reason", errorReason)
207		}
208	}
209
